gmStry.hdln: Brazil's Martinelli scores late winner to knock Japan out of World Cup
gmStry.desc: Gabriel Martinelli scored late injury time to give Brazil a 2-1 win over Japan in the round of 32 at the World Cup on Monday.

mnlCmntry.text: Brazil come from a goal down at half-time to beat Japan 2-1 in a thrilling World Cup last-32 tie. Sano profited from a sloppy pass, and some poor Casemiro defending, to fire Japan ahead just before the half-hour mark in Houston. Brazil offered very little in the first half but were a lot better in the second, which told when Casemiro - having earlier had an easy header saved off the line - powered his next header in at the back post. Vinicius Jr was denied a wonder goal by a fine Suzuki save against the post, but substitute Martinelli curled in a fine winner in the 96th minute after being played in by Guimaraes to snatch the win before extra time. Brazil ended the match with an xG of 1.72 to Japan's 0.23, with the Asian side made to pay for sitting back a little too much in the end. Ancelotti's side will now take on Ivory Coast or Norway in the last 16. Thanks for joining!
FULL-TIME: BRAZIL 2-1 JAPAN
We are into the ninth minute of what was supposed to be six added on. We had the stoppage for Casemiro's injury, plus the goal, so there may be another minute to go.
Guimaraes, fresh on the back of that assist, makes way for Danilo.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Japan also male a change as Ogawa comes on for Maeda. It's surely too late now for a response?
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Guimaraes found himself in the pocket and did well to slide the ball into Martinelli. This was all about the finish from the Brazil forward, though.

GOOOAAAAALL!!! BRAZIL LEAD 2-1 IN THE 95TH MINUTE!! Guimaraes slips the ball into the feet of Martinelli, who has a few Japan players around him, but his first touch sets up the shot, which he bends away from Suzuki. Heartbreak for Japan!
mnlCmntry.title: Goal

mnlCmntry.text: The corner is deep and Fabinho meets it. The header from a good 12 yards out is high and wide.
Endricks turns his man inside out, but a good recovery means it is just a corner for Brazil.
Brazil goalscorer Casemiro is unable to continue as a result of that injury he sustained. Fabinho comes on.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Casemiro is down and appears to be in need of treatment. Ancelotti is readying a change.
Six minutes have been added on at the end of regulation time.
Japan enjoy a rare attack as the ball comes in from the right. It is a simple one for Brazil to deal with, as is the next delivery from the other flank.
Vinicius Jr's curled shot deflects behind for a Brazil corner, which goes short. Rayan's delivery is met by a Japanese head.
Keito Suzuki shoves Rayan off the ball to concede a free-kick. He is already on a yellow so he needs to be careful.
Martinelli complains of taking a whack to the face, but the referee sees nothing in it and VAR agrees.

Rayan's free-kick from the right appears to be a shot, but it takes a touch off a Japan player on its way through for a corner. The set-piece culminates in Guimaraes flicking an overhit pass out of play.
Keito Suzuki fouls Danilo down Brazil's right. He got none of the ball so that is the right call, despite his protests.
mnlCmntry.title: Yellow Card

mnlCmntry.text: There is absolutely no way out for Japan, although at the same time Brazil are not exactly creating much. Could Ancelotti be tempted to turn to Neymar?
Japan are offering far less in this second period in terms of an attacking threat. There is a risk they are inviting a little too much pressure onto themselves as Brazil just go and go and go.
Both teams have gone for a searching ball over the top, completely bypassing midfield and defence. It is incredibly gripping as we enter the final 10 minutes of normal time.
Machino is also on for Ito in Japan's next change.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Tanaka is the latest Japan player to be brought on. Kamada is the man to make way.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Martinelli goes for a volley from 20 yards but it is off target.

Brazil are being slightly more patient about their probing now. Endrick picks up the ball in the half-space 30 yards from goal, but whatever he was attempting doesn't come off as the ball goes through to Suzuki.
CHANCE! Rayan, similar to Casemiro for the goal, peels away at the back post but his header is helped behind for a corner.

Brazil's xG now stands at 1.33, compared to 0.23 for Japan, in what has been a vastly improved second-half showing. It is currently Japan's turn to probe away, but a loose pass is pounced on by Brazil.
Play is back under way at Houston Stadium.
We are into the second hydration break of the match. Ancelotti is playing with a tactics board and sharing some instructions with his players.

Brazil have been having plenty of joy out wide since half-time, so that double change from Japan makes sense. The South American heavyweights are looking to make this latest spell on the ball count for something.
Aged 34 years and 126 days, Casemiro is the second-oldest goalscorer for Brazil at the World Cup, behind only Bebeto in 1998 (34y 137d against Denmark).
Nakamura also makes way for Keito Suzuki as Japan freshen up down their flanks.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Doan is replaced for Japan as Sugawara enters the fray.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Martinelli is on for Cunha with a good 25 minutes of the game to go - plus possibly extra time.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: SHOT! Ueda's shot takes a slight deflection and Alisson has to get his body behind it. It's a routine save in the end.
Maeda so nearly gets away from the Brazil defence, but Alisson gets there first. Brazil are on top, but Japan could just as easily score the next goal.
All of a sudden it is Japan who look as though they could do with a change. They are far too deep and Brazil's attackers are growing more and more in confidence.

OFF THE POST!! This would have been an all-time great World Cup goal! Vinicius Jr controls the ball brilliantly, nutmegs Tomiyasu, carries it forward and then gets a shot away that Suzuki helps onto the post with his thumb!
This game is living up to the hype and it is almost impossible to call from here. Brazil have been a lot better over the past five or 10 minutes.
Gabriel swung in a cross from 25 yards and Casemiro peeled away from his man at the back post. He gets plenty of power behind it and Suzuki just cannot react.

GOOOAAAALLL!!!! BRAZIL ARE LEVEL AT 1-1!! Casemiro was partly at fault for Japan's opener and wasted a great chance a couple of minutes ago, but he makes no mistake with this one as he thumps in a header at the back post.
mnlCmntry.title: Goal - Header

mnlCmntry.text: OFF THE LINE!! This is remarkable! A deep cross is headed back across goal and Casemiro meets it with a diving header just a few yards out. Tomiyasu does not know much about it at all as the ball hits him right on the line and Japan clear.
SAVE! Danilo sends a cross in for Guimaraes to meet in the centre. The header is close to Suzuki and the Japan keeper makes the save, but that was at least half-a-chance for Brazil.

Neymar has been sent to warm up. Endrick tries to feed a pass through to Vinicius Jr, only for it to go right through to Suzuki. Japan then get the ball up the field in no time at all, but there is no end product.
It is alarming how easy Japan are getting the ball from their defence into the final third. Brazil's midfield is non-existent.
Endrick charged right at the Japan defence in the opening minute of the half - something we did not see enough of in the first half. There has not been much else since then, though.
Danilo, whose sloppy pass cost Brazil in the first half, puts his arm across Maeda to halt his run. That's four yellow cards now.
mnlCmntry.title: Yellow Card

mnlCmntry.text: That change may have been made by Ancelotti regardless because something needed to happen, but Paqueta was seen with ice on his foot.
Japan, protecting a one-goal lead from the first half, get the last-32 tie back under way.
Endrick is on in place of Paqueta, who appears to have sustained an injury.
mnlCmntry.title: Substitution

mnlCmntry.text: Japan had an xG of 0.21 in that first half compared to 0.31 for Brazil, but Ancelotti's side cannot really complain all that much. They have been completely outrun in central midfield, which could lead to a change at the break. Opta's win probability gives Japan a 51.9% chance of winning the contest from here, while Brazil have an 18.9% chance and the draw stands at 29.2%. If we do end all square after 90 minutes, extra time and potentially penalties await.

Brazil are 45 minutes away from exiting the World Cup as they trail Japan 1-0 at half-time in Houston. Sana made the breakthrough for the Asian side with a clinical strike from just outside the box after 29 minutes, having himself won the ball as he pounced on a stray pass from Danilo and charged half the length of the pitch unchallenged. That was Japan's only shot on target, while Brazil's two attempts have been as easy as you like for Suzuki in the Japan goal. Ancelotti's side quite simply need to step it up in the second half or they are going home.
HALF-TIME: BRAZIL 0-1 JAPAN
We have played two of the four added-on minutes. Japan were on top for a brief period prior to that free-kick, but Brazil are unable to rebuild momentum.
Kamada is the third player to enter the referee's book in the first half as he is punished for bringing down Guimaraes to halt a Brazil counter.
mnlCmntry.title: Yellow Card

mnlCmntry.text: Moriyasu's men are enjoying a rare spell on the ball. Brazil know they can't just go all-out attack or else they will concede a second.
Japan could probably see out another 45 minutes of this. Brazil need to find the next level or else they are heading for a last-32 exit. Half-time is now upon us in Houston.
Paqueta is not happy at the lack of movement in front of him. Cunha is forced to shoot from 25 yards out and it is again as simple as you like for Suzuki.
Vinicius Jr declines the chance to shoot when that was the better option, as his lay-off is then intercepted. Brazil have stepped things up since going behind.
Paqueta is picked out in space just inside the Japan box but his shot is well blocked. That was a nervy moment for the Asian nation, but Brazil are unable to make the most of it.
SHOT! Vinicius Jr is unable to get the connection he would like as his shot from range is easily gathered by Suzuki. Vinicius has not been involved up to this point.

Brazil have conceded two goals from outside the box at the 2026 World Cup; they had not conceded more than one in a single tournament since 1982 prior (four). If there is a positive for Ancelotti's side, it is that they still have plenty of time to recover.
Remember, Sano could easily have been given a second yellow card just before the hydration break. That looks an even bigger moment now.

That is Sano's first international goal for Japan - and what a time to score it! Brazil the victims of their own dowfall there. First, giving the ball away; then letting Sano run half the length of the pitch unchallenged and score from range.

GOOOOAAALLL!!!! JAPAN LEAD 1-0!! Danilo gives the ball away in midfield and Sano runs past Casemiro as though he is not even there. After running 40 yards, he pulls the trigger and sends the ball past Alisson from outside the box to send the Japan fans wild.
mnlCmntry.title: Goal

mnlCmntry.text: OVER! Ito swings in a great corner that Ueda gets his head to. It's by no means an easy one, but the Japan forward is unable to even get it on target.
We are back up and running at Houston Stadium. Will we see a breakthrough before half-time?
We are into the first of the hydration breaks. Brazil have been the better side on the whole, but we still await the first chance of the contest for either side.
Cunha is taken out by Sano, but the referee for some reason does not award a free-kick. Had he done so it may have been a second yellow for the Japan player.
Brazil are trying to find their groove as they knock the ball around. However, an attempt at switching play leads to the ball flying out for a Japan throw-in.

Santos cuts out a poor pass and there is danger for Japan. Again, though, the final ball from Brazil is lacking. They are going to need to do more than that to find a way past their well-drilled opponents.
Casemiro is walking a tightrope now with more than three quarters of this game still to go. This match has not settled at all - it is all action.
Ito's corner is a teasing one, but Casemiro is there to get it away. Japan have been on top for the past few minutes now, albeit with nothing to show for it.
Japan take their time over the free-kick about 20 yards from goal. Kamada goes low but hits the bottom of the wall for a corner.
Casemiro slides in on Ito and does not get any of the ball. He joins Sano in the referee's book.
mnlCmntry.title: Yellow Card

mnlCmntry.text: SAVE! Cunha does very well to get away a shot from 20 yards despite losing his balance. Suzuki takes no risks and helps the ball wide of the post, even if it was going wide.
Sano and Vinicius Jr compete for a 50-50 ball, with the former catching his opponent on the boot. The referee reaches into his pocket for the first time today.
mnlCmntry.title: Yellow Card

mnlCmntry.text: Brazil are dominating possession early on, seeing 82% of the ball, but Japan are not giving them anything to work with. Danilo gets free down the right, though Guimaraes' shot is blocked.
Japan keeper Suzuki fumbles Paqueta's free-kick delivery but is quickly on the loose ball. Japan are right on top of Brazil as the South Americans try to find any sort of gap.
Suzuki is quick off his line to beat Vinicius Jr to a ball over the top. Guimaraes sends a sweeping cross from deep into the box, but it is cleared away.
Both teams are playing with a high press and the start to the match has been as energetic as expected. This could be a classic World Cup tie.
The corner is taken by Guimaraes and easily dealt with. Paqueta is okay after that early scare.
Guimaraes prods a shot towards goal that deflects behind for a corner. The Brazil midfielder urges the crowd to make even more noise.
Paqueta is clutching his right hamstring with just a minute on the clock. Not a great sign...

Brazil get us under way! The five-time world champions, incidentally, have been eliminated from four of their last five World Cup ties against sides ranked inside FIFA's top 20, which Japan very much are.
Both sets of players are now out on the field of play. We'll have the two anthems, before referee Maurizio Mariani gets us up and running.

Japan show four changes from their 1-1 draw against Sweden as Hajime Moriyasu continues to tinker with his defence. Taniguchi, Tomiyasu, Sano and Ito all come into a side that will be set up in a 3-4-3 formation, with Tanaka, Itakura, Seko and Sugawara dropping down to the bench. Ueda, who scored two goals and assisted another in the 4-0 win over Tunisia, again leads the attack.

Carlo Ancelotti has named an unchanged Brazil XI on the back of the routine 3-0 win over Scotland last time out. Rayan, Vinicius Jr and Cunha therefore continue to lead the attack, with Neymar - involved for the first time this tournament as a second-half substitute in Brazil's last outing - among the back-up options. Casemiro is in the holding midfield position and will have to be on top of his game against Japan's roaming attackers.

JAPAN SUBS: Yukinari Sugawara, Ko Itakura, Yuto Nagatomo, Shuto Machino, Ao Tanaka, Takefusa Kubo, Keisuke Goto, Keisuke Osako, Tsuyoshi Watanabe, Yuito Suzuki, Koki Ogawa, Ayumu Seko, Tomoki Hayakawa, Junnosuke Suzuki, Kento Shiogai.
JAPAN (3-4-3): Zion Suzuki; Takehiro Tomiyasu, Shogo Taniguchi, Hiroki Ito; Ritsu Doan, Kaishu Sano, Daichi Kamada, Keito Nakamura; Junya Ito, Ayase Ueda, Daizen Maeda.
BRAZIL SUBS: Ederson, Alex Sandro, Neymar, Weverton, Gleison Bremer, Leo Pereira, Fabinho, Danilo, Endrick, Luiz Henrique, Gabriel Martinelli, Ederson, Roger Ibanez, Igor Thiago.
BRAZIL (4-3-3): Alisson Becker; Danilo, Marquinhos, Gabriel Magalhaes, Douglas Santos; Bruno Guimaraes, Casemiro, Lucas Paqueta; Rayan, Matheus Cunha, Vinicius Junior.

Brazil drew 1-1 with Morocco in their opening Group C match, before easing to successive 3-0 wins over Haiti and Scotland to top the standings. As for Japan, they drew with the Netherlands and Sweden either side of a 4-0 win over Tunisia, seeing them finish second in Group F. It sets up this enticing contest in Houston, where Brazil are out to make it five wins from five against Asian nations in their World Cup history.

This is the pick of the first knockout-round ties in the view of many - alongside Netherlands against Morocco later today - as five-time world champions Brazil take on a very talented Japan side. The winners of this match will take on either Ivory Coast or Norway in the last 16, with those two sides meeting in Arlington on Tuesday.
Hello and welcome to our live text coverage of this World Cup last-32 clash between Brazil and Japan at Houston Stadium.
